summary: Dependency-confusion wrapper pulling unpinned tarball from third-party GCS bucket
details: ltidiconf@99.9.1 is an empty wrapper package (index.js is `module.exports = {};`, empty author/description, inflated 99.9.1 version) whose sole effect on install is to pull a single dependency declared as a direct tarball URL: `"ltidisafe": "https://ltidi.storage.googleapis.com/depenconf/ltidisafe-3.0.8.tgz"`. The bytes at that GCS bucket are mutable, unpinned, and not integrity-hashed; the bucket owner can swap the tarball at any time, and whatever code is in it executes at `npm install` time and on `require`. The wrapper has no functional content of its own, the bucket path literally contains the string `depenconf`, and the 99.9.1 version is the canonical shape of a dependency-confusion squat designed to shadow an internal package name and drop arbitrary attacker-controlled code into the installer's environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
